spec_match.rationale: The live dialog now uses command="request-close" and command="close" attributes, with JavaScript limited to cancel interception, logging, and fallback behavior.

interactive.rationale: Opening the dialog, typing into the form, toggling the guard, and using the command buttons all produce live cancel/close event output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept matches the feature page promise: a declarative cancel button that can be intercepted before the dialog closes.

edge_cases.rationale: The page visibly covers clean close, dirty-form preventDefault, save-and-close bypass, requestClose() fallback, and missing command-attribute support.

references.rationale: The see-also section cites https://chromestatus.com/feature/5592399713402880 and the request-close spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: The updated status panel and buttons use shared design variables and keep background and text colors paired.

summary: Declarative Confirm Flow now runs valid JavaScript, uses real invoker command attributes for request-close and close, demonstrates guarded and unguarded branches, and cites the correct ChromeStatus feature.
